drillingThunderRelease.title: Scrap Mechanic 1.0 Release Date and Drilling Thunder
drillingThunderRelease.intro: Scrap Mechanic leaves Early Access on July 24, 2026 with the free 1.0 Drilling Thunder update. Here is the official release timeline and every confirmed feature, from underground drilling and redesigned terrain to new bots, reworked raids, weapons, building parts, and graphical upgrades.

timeline.title: 1.0 & Drilling Thunder Launch
timeline.description: Scrap Mechanic 1.0 and the Drilling Thunder update release together, ending Early Access with the complete Survival, Creative, and Challenge experience.

timeline.title: Free for Every Owner
timeline.description: Drilling Thunder is a free update for everyone who already owns Scrap Mechanic on Steam, with no separate purchase required.

timeline.title: End of Early Access
timeline.description: Survival, Creative, and Challenge modes move out of Early Access into the finished 1.0 build with the redesigned progression.

features.name: Underground Drilling
features.description: Drill into all-new underground layers packed with fresh resources, new mining threats, and deeper exploration routes.

features.name: Redesigned Terrain
features.description: Overhauled world generation and biomes change how Survival maps look and play across every new save.

features.name: New Enemy Bots
features.description: Face the flying Minerbot, explosive Red Totebot, jumping Yellow Totebot, and amphibious Blue Totebot.

features.name: Reworked Raids
features.description: Raids now start immediately when triggered, with smarter enemy pathing and improved difficulty scaling.

features.name: New Weapons & Parts
features.description: Expand your arsenal and toolbox with additional weapons and building parts for combat and engineering.

features.name: Graphical Upgrades
features.description: Visual and lighting improvements bring the farming planet up to a modern, polished look.

beginnerGuide.title: Scrap Mechanic Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Start Survival the right way. Follow the opening quests from finding the Master Battery to activating the crashed ship and building your first vehicle, while learning controls, inventory management, food, water, the Lift, and the Connection Tool.

steps.title: Find the Master Battery
steps.description: Search the starting crash site and nearby points of interest for the Master Battery, the key that powers your first vehicle and base.

steps.title: Activate the Crashed Ship
steps.description: Use the Master Battery on the crashed ship to reveal the Mechanic Station map marker and unlock your earliest crafting options.

steps.title: Manage Controls, Food, and Water
steps.description: Master movement, the Lift, and inventory management. Keep food and water stocked so low health and hunger never catch you off guard.

steps.title: Learn the Lift and Connection Tool
steps.description: Use the Lift to position parts and the Connection Tool to wire seats, engines, bearings, and switches into working machines.

steps.title: Build Your First Vehicle
steps.description: Assemble a simple stable car with a seat, engine, wheels, and bearings, then test it at low power before adding more moving parts.

steps.title: Set Early Resource Priorities
steps.description: Gather wood, stone, and metal first, then push toward a Mechanic Station and a Packing Station for steady progression.

quickTips: Always carry a Sledgehammer, your main tool for breaking blocks and harvesting resources.
Keep the Lift nearby when building; it makes placing and rotating parts much easier.
Store spare Master Batteries safely, because losing power can strand your vehicle far from base.
Water and food drain over time, so stock up before long exploration or farming trips.

survivalMap.title: Scrap Mechanic Survival Map and Locations
survivalMap.intro: Navigate the Survival world with confidence. Learn where to find Mechanic Stations, Packing Stations, the Trader, warehouses, ruins, and resource biomes, plus a recommended exploration route and how generated worlds change in the 1.0 update.

locations.name: Mechanic Station
locations.description: A key early landmark that unlocks crafting benches and points you toward the next progression steps on the map.

locations.name: Packing Station
locations.description: Pack harvested crops into crates for sale, turning steady farming output into a reliable income stream.

locations.name: The Trader
locations.description: A merchant who buys packed produce and sells useful supplies, seeds, and upgrades for your base.

locations.name: Warehouses
locations.description: Large structures packed with loot, Component Kits, and dangerous Tapebots guarding every floor.

locations.name: Ruins
locations.description: Scattered ruins hide resources, parts, and clues that make early exploration far more rewarding.

locations.name: Resource Biomes
locations.description: Forests, fields, mountains, and lakes each supply different wood, stone, metal, and food materials.

locations.name: Underground Layers
locations.description: Drilling Thunder adds drillable underground areas with fresh resources and new mining enemies below the surface.

routeSteps: Start at the crash site and grab the Master Battery
Reach a Mechanic Station to unlock crafting
Open the Packing Station and meet the Trader
Push toward warehouses for Component Kits

craftingRecipes.title: Scrap Mechanic Crafting Recipes and Craftbots
craftingRecipes.intro: Organize your crafting around the Scrap Mechanic family of stations. Learn what each Craftbot produces, which materials matter, how station upgrades unlock new recipes, and which Component Kit priorities keep your progression moving.

stations.name: Mini Craftbot
stations.description: A compact early crafting bot that handles your first basic recipes before you reach a full station.

stations.name: Craftbot
stations.description: The main crafting hub that turns raw resources and Component Kits into blocks, parts, and tools.

stations.name: Refinebot
stations.description: Processes raw mined and harvested materials into refined inputs used by other stations.

stations.name: Cookbot
stations.description: Combines crops and ingredients into cooked food that restores more hunger than raw produce.

stations.name: Dressbot
stations.description: Crafts clothing and cosmetic items so you can customize your character's look.

componentPriorities: Upgrade the Craftbot first to unlock higher-tier recipes.
Prioritize Component Kits that unlock engines, bearings, and controllers.
Keep the Refinebot fed with a steady supply of raw material.
Build automated crafting chains once you have stable power and storage.

vehiclesAndBuilding.title: Scrap Mechanic Vehicles and Building Guide
vehiclesAndBuilding.intro: Successful creations start with a rigid frame, correctly oriented bearings, and balanced weight. Use the Connection Tool to link seats, engines, controllers, switches, sensors, and powered joints, then test each system at low speed before increasing power or adding more moving parts.

blueprints.name: Stable Starter Car
blueprints.bestFor: Exploration, early resource runs, and learning vehicle controls
buildSteps: Build a wide rectangular chassis with the seat near its center.
Mount the front wheels on steering bearings and the driven wheels on powered bearings.
Connect the seat to the steering bearings and the engine to the powered wheel bearings.
Use the Connection Tool arrows to correct wheels that rotate or steer the wrong way.
Begin with low engine power and increase it after the car can accelerate without flipping.
blueprints.tuning: Keep heavy engines, batteries, and storage close to the floor. A longer wheelbase and wider track improve stability, while suspension helps maintain wheel contact on uneven terrain.
failureChecks: Narrow chassis with a high center of mass
Steering bearings connected to the engine

blueprints.name: Off-Road Cargo Hauler
blueprints.bestFor: Transporting crates, fuel, crops, and building materials
buildSteps: Place the cargo area between the front and rear axles instead of behind the vehicle.
Use suspension on all wheels and leave enough clearance for it to compress.
Spread containers evenly across the chassis and secure loose cargo inside walls or rails.
Power multiple wheels when the vehicle must climb hills or carry heavy loads.
blueprints.tuning: Test the hauler both empty and fully loaded. Reduce speed or widen the chassis when the vehicle becomes unstable after adding cargo.
failureChecks: Cargo weight concentrated at the rear
Engine power too low for the loaded vehicle

blueprints.name: Tree Harvester
blueprints.bestFor: Cutting trees and collecting wood more efficiently
buildSteps: Mount one or more Saw Blades on powered bearings at the front of a stable vehicle.
Position the blades low enough to contact tree trunks without striking the ground.
Protect the driver's seat and wheels from falling logs.
Place a Resource Collector where refined wood pieces can be gathered.
Approach trees slowly so the blades stay in contact instead of pushing the vehicle sideways.
blueprints.tuning: Use moderate rotation speed and a heavy, wide chassis. Fast blades on a light vehicle often cause bouncing, steering loss, or rollovers.
failureChecks: Vehicle too light for the cutting force
Collector positioned outside the material path

blueprints.name: Rock Mining Rig
blueprints.bestFor: Breaking stone formations and gathering metal-bearing resources
buildSteps: Mount rotating drills on a reinforced front assembly.
Use pistons when the drill needs controlled forward pressure or height adjustment.
Connect drill bearings to an engine and piston controls to separate switches.
Place a Resource Collector close to the broken resource pieces.
Advance slowly and let the drill break the node instead of ramming it.
blueprints.tuning: A low-speed, high-control setup is easier to keep aligned. Counterweights or a wider rear section prevent the drill assembly from pulling the vehicle forward or sideways.
failureChecks: Drills spinning away from the target
Front assembly heavier than the rear

blueprints.name: Vanilla Utility Boat
blueprints.bestFor: Crossing lakes and moving supplies between shorelines
coreParts: Thrusters or another water-safe propulsion system
buildSteps: Build a wide symmetrical hull and test whether it floats level before adding machinery.
Place the seat, fuel, and cargo near the center of the hull.
Mount propulsion evenly on both sides or along the centerline.
Use steering bearings, independently controlled propulsion, or movable thrust to turn.
Keep important controls and cargo above the normal waterline.
blueprints.tuning: Add weight in small amounts and retest after every change. Uneven equipment placement causes listing, while narrow hulls become unstable during sharp turns.
failureChecks: Heavy cargo placed at one end

blueprints.name: Controller and Logic Machine
blueprints.bestFor: Doors, elevators, folding vehicles, walkers, traps, and automated production
buildSteps: Use a Controller to assign target angles and movement sequences to connected bearings.
Use Pistons for straight extension, lifting, or clamping movements.
Connect Sensors to detect players, bots, crops, or moving objects.
Use Logic Gates to require multiple conditions or invert an input.
Add Timers when actions must happen in a fixed order instead of simultaneously.
Test one moving joint at a time before connecting the complete sequence.
blueprints.tuning: Slow controller speed and piston movement during testing. Leave clearance around every rotating or extending section and use separate switches for reset or emergency shutdown.
failureChecks: Bearing rotation blocked by another part
Controller steps using the wrong rotation direction
Too many moving bodies colliding at once

farmingAndRaid.title: Scrap Mechanic Farming and Raid Defense
farmingAndRaid.intro: Place Soil Bags on suitable ground, plant one seed in each plot, and supply water until the crop matures. Fertilizer accelerates growth, while every planted crop adds to the farm's raid value under the current Survival system.

stages.whatToDo: Place Soil Bags on flat ground, plant one seed per plot, and water each crop with a Water Bucket. Apply Fertilizer when faster growth matters more than conserving supplies.
stages.keyData: Crops must be watered to progress. Fertilizer speeds growth but does not remove the need for water.
stages.raidRisk: Keep the first farm small while gathering building materials and weapons.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Use terrain, a simple wall, and a clear escape path.

stages.whatToDo: Use these crops for early food, trading, and learning the raid system.
stages.keyData: Redbeet: 1 crop value; Carrot: 1 crop value; Tomato: 1 crop value.
stages.raidRisk: Ten of these crops reach the current minimum total crop value required to trigger a raid.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Build a perimeter that funnels bots toward one side of the farm.

stages.system: Potato, Cotton, Banana, Blueberry, and Orange
stages.whatToDo: Grow Potatoes for Spudgun ammunition, Cotton for crafting, and fruit for food or produce trading.
stages.keyData: Potato: 1.5 crop value; Cotton: 1.5; Banana: 2; Blueberry: 2; Orange: 2.
stages.raidRisk: A smaller number of these crops can trigger the same raid threshold as a larger basic farm.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Upgrade vulnerable walls and add elevated firing positions.

stages.whatToDo: Use these late-game crops for advanced food and trading after obtaining their seeds through warehouse progression.
stages.keyData: Broccoli: 3 crop value; Pineapple: 3 crop value. Both count as high-value crops in the current raid system.
stages.raidRisk: Large high-value farms can introduce Tapebots and Farmbots into advanced raids.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Use strong materials, protected firing lanes, and multiple fallback barriers.

stages.whatToDo: Check the raid warning before leaving the farm and finish repairs before the attack begins.
stages.keyData: A raid is triggered when the local crop total reaches at least 10. The raid level is calculated when farming is detected, so harvesting crops afterward does not cancel the scheduled attack.
stages.raidRisk: Farming on consecutive days increases raid intensity. Breaking the streak lets the escalation reset.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Keep spare blocks, ammunition, food, and a bed away from the outer wall.

stages.system: Water Container, Vacuum Pump, and Water Cannon
stages.whatToDo: Collect water into a Water Container and use mechanically aimed Water Cannons to cover rows of crops.
stages.keyData: Controllers, bearings, or pistons can move a Water Cannon across several rows. Timers and Logic Gates can coordinate repeated watering cycles.
stages.raidRisk: Automation makes larger farms practical, but larger planted areas create stronger raids.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Protect pumps, containers, controllers, and exposed pipes behind the main wall.

stages.whatToDo: Create narrow entrances that force bots into predictable paths. Use rotating blades, crushers, traps, or mechanically aimed Spudguns to support manual combat.
stages.keyData: Bots attack crops and structures, so defense systems should sit outside the crop area with enough clearance to move.
stages.raidRisk: Complex traps can jam when bot bodies, loose blocks, or damaged parts enter the mechanism.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Add a manual override and keep a clear firing line from inside the base.

stages.system: UV Grow Beds and redesigned attacks
stages.whatToDo: Use electricity-powered UV Grow Beds to farm underground or on moving creations, then protect them as carefully as outdoor crops.
stages.keyData: The redesigned raid system starts attacks immediately after they are triggered, improves difficulty scaling, and gives enemies smarter path selection. Newer bot variants can also appear.
stages.raidRisk: Old defenses built around waiting until midnight or exploiting weak pathfinding require redesigning.
stages.defenseUpgrade: Create several defended routes, protect crops on all sides, and prepare weapons for melee, ranged, explosive, and flying bots.

botsAndCombat.title: Scrap Mechanic Bots and Combat Guide
botsAndCombat.intro: Do not fight every bot the same way. Melee bots can be separated and kited, Tapebots demand cover, Farmbots should be attacked from range, and the 1.0 variants introduce jumping, underwater, explosive, slowing, and flying threats.

bots.name: Green Totebot
bots.combatStrategy: Let the Totebot approach, step away from its swing, and counter with the Sledgehammer. Avoid letting several Totebots surround you.

bots.name: Haybot
bots.attackType: Pitchfork poke and heavier melee swing
bots.combatStrategy: Walk backward while attacking and separate Haybots before committing to melee. Use water, terrain, or obstacles to interrupt groups and create room.

bots.name: Blue and Red Tapebots
bots.attackType: Rapid tape projectiles or slower explosive projectiles
bots.combatStrategy: Fight from cover and expose only enough of your position to fire. Never rush through warehouse tape without checking the room, and move away from Red Tapebot explosive shots.

bots.name: Farmbot
bots.attackType: Scythe attacks, kicks, and pesticide-based area damage
bots.combatStrategy: Maintain range and use large obstacles to block charges. Do not stay inside pesticide clouds or attempt extended Sledgehammer combat against a healthy Farmbot.

bots.name: Minerbot
bots.attackType: Flying rock spray followed by a dangerous crash when destroyed
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the official bot reveal
bots.combatStrategy: Keep moving while tracking the bot in the air. Shoot from an angle that prevents the damaged Minerbot from crashing directly onto you.

bots.name: Yellow Totebot
bots.attackType: Jumping stomp that can smash through floors and ceilings
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the official bot reveal
bots.combatStrategy: Do not rely on a single floor or low roof for protection. Move sideways when it jumps and keep important controls away from exposed ceilings.

bots.name: Blue Totebot
bots.attackType: Fast underwater torpedo attack and rapid pursuit on land
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the official bot reveal
bots.combatStrategy: Do not assume water is a safe retreat. Leave the water quickly, gain distance, and fight from stable ground where its approach is easier to track.

bots.name: Red Totebot
bots.attackType: Explosive suicide charge that can ignite nearby material
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the official bot reveal
bots.combatStrategy: Shoot it before it reaches walls, vehicles, or crops. Separate it from other bots so its explosion damages enemies instead of opening a path into your base.

bots.name: Green and Yellow Tapebots
bots.attackType: Weaponized bubble plastic or damaging ink that slows movement
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the official bot reveal
bots.combatStrategy: Prioritize Yellow Tapebots before their slowing ink limits your movement. Green Tapebots are less threatening alone but become dangerous when several continuously pressure the same position.

bots.name: Trashbot
notableDrops: No unique loot listed in the latest official update
bots.combatStrategy: Use space, cover, and sustained ranged damage. Avoid entering a confined room without a retreat route or enough ammunition to finish the encounter.

modsAndWorkshop.title: Scrap Mechanic Mods and Steam Workshop
modsAndWorkshop.intro: Steam Workshop content is installed by subscribing to an item while signed into Steam. Scrap Mechanic supports ordinary blueprints, Blocks and Parts mods, custom Creative worlds, and complete Custom Games with their own scripts, tools, enemies, terrain, and progression.

workshop.name: Workshop Blueprints
workshop.whatItAdds: Vehicles, machines, buildings, logic systems, and decorative creations saved by other players.
installation: Open the Scrap Mechanic Steam Workshop.
Select a blueprint and press Subscribe.
Launch the game after Steam finishes downloading the item.
Open the Lift's creation browser in a mode that supports blueprint loading.
Choose the subscribed creation and place it on the Lift.
workshop.compatibility: A blueprint that uses modded blocks requires every listed Blocks and Parts dependency.
workshop.precautions: Check the blueprint's size and required mods before loading it into an important world. Very large or highly complex creations can reduce performance.

workshop.name: Blocks and Parts Mods
workshop.whatItAdds: New blocks, engines, wheels, interactive parts, logic components, tools, weapons, effects, and decorative pieces.
installation: Subscribe to the main mod and every dependency shown on its Workshop page.
Enable the required mods when creating or loading a compatible modded world.
Restart the game when newly subscribed content does not appear immediately.
workshop.compatibility: Multiplayer participants must have access to the same enabled content. Removing a required mod can leave missing parts or stop a creation from loading correctly.
workshop.precautions: Keep a list of required mods for long-term worlds and avoid unsubscribing from dependencies while those worlds still use their parts.

workshop.name: Custom Worlds
workshop.whatItAdds: Community-built terrain, roads, structures, tiles, challenges, and exploration spaces.
installation: Subscribe to the world through the Workshop's Worlds category.
Open the game's world or Creative selection screen.
Select the subscribed custom world and review its required mods.
Create a separate save for the first test.
workshop.compatibility: Custom Worlds can depend on terrain assets, Blocks and Parts mods, or additional Workshop packages.
workshop.precautions: Do not overwrite an established save when testing a newly downloaded world. Keep the original world and test copy separate.

workshop.name: Custom Games
workshop.whatItAdds: Modded Survival experiences or standalone games with custom scripts, quests, tools, enemies, terrain, and rules.
installation: Subscribe to the Custom Game and its dependencies.
Open the Game Mode selection screen.
Select Custom Games in the upper-right area.
Choose the downloaded game and create a new save.
workshop.compatibility: The host selects the Custom Game and controls the session. All players should finish downloading the same dependencies before joining.
workshop.precautions: Use a dedicated save for each Custom Game because its scripts, inventory, and world rules may not match standard Survival or Creative mode.

workshop.name: Multiplayer Mod Setup
workshop.whatItAdds: Shared access to modded worlds, creations, and complete Custom Games.
installation: Let the host choose the world and enabled mod set.
Share the Workshop collection or dependency list with every player.
Wait for Steam to finish all downloads before joining.
Restart Steam or Scrap Mechanic when a dependency remains unavailable.
workshop.compatibility: Different mod versions, missing dependencies, or removed Workshop items can prevent joining or cause missing content.
workshop.precautions: Do not change the enabled mod list during an active campaign unless the mod author provides migration instructions.

workshop.name: Save and Update Safety
workshop.whatItAdds: A safer process for testing updates, experimental branches, and large mod collections.
installation: Close Scrap Mechanic before copying save files.
Back up the Scrap Mechanic user save directory before major game or mod updates.
Test updated content in a copied world.
Keep stable-version and test-branch saves separate.
workshop.compatibility: Content created with a newer Mod Tool, test branch, or incompatible game version may not load in an older version.
workshop.precautions: Never open an important stable save on an experimental branch without a backup. Windows saves and automatic backups are stored under the Scrap Mechanic folder inside AppData/Roaming/Axolot Games.

workshop.name: Creating Lua-Powered Mods
workshop.whatItAdds: Custom parts, tools, weapons, NPCs, projectiles, melee attacks, harvestables, effects, game logic, worlds, and procedural terrain.
installation: Install and open the official Scrap Mechanic Mod Tool through Steam.
Create the appropriate Blocks and Parts, World, or Custom Game project.
Define content through JSON files and use Lua scripts for behavior.
Start Scrap Mechanic with the -dev launch option while scripting to access the debug console and script hot reload.
Test client and server behavior before publishing the project to Workshop.
workshop.compatibility: Scrap Mechanic uses Lua 5.1 and separates server-side simulation from client-side visuals, audio, and input. Workshop dependencies can be declared through the Mod Tool.
workshop.precautions: Use unique identifiers, validate JSON, avoid unrestricted assumptions about client access, and test multiplayer networking before releasing an update.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Scrap Mechanic

questions.question: What is Scrap Mechanic?
questions.answer: Scrap Mechanic is a physics-driven survival sandbox by Axolot Games where you build vehicles and machines, explore a farming planet, craft resources, and defend against Farmbots. It is available on Steam for Windows.

questions.question: When does Scrap Mechanic 1.0 release?
questions.answer: Scrap Mechanic 1.0 and the Drilling Thunder update release on July 24, 2026 as a free update that ends Early Access for every existing owner.

questions.question: Is Scrap Mechanic multiplayer?
questions.answer: Yes. Scrap Mechanic supports cooperative play for up to several players across Survival, Creative, and Challenge modes, so you can build, farm, and explore together.

questions.question: What are the game modes in Scrap Mechanic?
questions.answer: Scrap Mechanic has three main modes: Survival, Creative, and Challenge. Creative Mode includes more than 400 building parts for unrestricted engineering.

questions.question: Where can I play Scrap Mechanic?
questions.answer: Scrap Mechanic is available on Steam for Windows. You can buy it from the official Scrap Mechanic Steam store page.

questions.question: How do raids work in Scrap Mechanic?
questions.answer: In Scrap Mechanic Survival, farming raises your farm's crop value. Once it reaches the threshold, a raid triggers and enemies attack your farm, so walls, traps, and weapons are essential.

cta.title: Ready to Master Scrap Mechanic?
cta.description: Join the r/ScrapMechanic community for vehicle builds, mods, and survival tips, or jump straight into the game on Steam.

builds.whatIsDescription: Builds are player-engineered vehicles and machines assembled from chassis, bearings, engines, wheels, controllers, and logic parts using the Connection Tool.

whySteps.step1Description: Link seats, engines, bearings, and switches into working machines
whySteps.step2Description: Keep heavy parts low and the chassis wide for stable handling
whySteps.step3Description: Use controllers, sensors, timers, and logic gates for advanced creations

crafting.whatIsDescription: Crafting in Scrap Mechanic turns raw resources and Component Kits into blocks, parts, tools, food, and cosmetics through the Craftbot, Refinebot, Cookbot, and Dressbot.

whySteps.step1Description: Upgrade the Craftbot first to unlock higher-tier recipes
whySteps.step2Description: Spend Component Kits on engines, bearings, and controllers

updates.metaTitle: Scrap Mechanic Updates - 1.0 and Drilling Thunder News
updates.metaDescription: Track the Scrap Mechanic release timeline, including the 1.0 launch and Drilling Thunder update with new bots, raids, and graphical upgrades.
updates.whatIsDescription: Updates cover official announcements, version changes, and release dates, with stable gameplay guides moved back into the relevant content categories.

whySteps.step1Description: Follow the 1.0 and Drilling Thunder launch schedule
whySteps.step2Description: Discover new bots, raids, weapons, and building parts
whySteps.step3Description: Prepare bases and farms for the redesigned systems

guides.title: Scrap Mechanic Guides
guides.description: Beginner guides covering controls, tools, keyboard shortcuts, console commands, and common mistakes across all game modes.
guides.metaTitle: Scrap Mechanic Guides - Controls, Tools, and Console Commands
guides.metaDescription: Learn Scrap Mechanic basics with guides on controls, tools, keyboard shortcuts, and console commands for Survival, Creative, and Challenge mode.
guides.whatIsDescription: Guides cover the universal basics every mechanic needs: movement and controls, tool functions, keyboard shortcuts, and console commands that work across Survival, Creative, and Challenge mode.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn movement, the lift, the spraycan, and the welding tool
whySteps.step2Description: Use keyboard shortcuts to build, rotate, and paint faster
whySteps.step3Description: Spawn items and adjust settings with creative commands

farming.title: Scrap Mechanic Farming
farming.description: Farming guides covering crops, watering, raid levels, packing stations, and trading with the Trader.
farming.metaTitle: Scrap Mechanic Farming - Crops, Raids, and Trading
farming.metaDescription: Master Scrap Mechanic farming with guides on crops, watering, automated irrigation, raid levels, crop defense, and trading for crates.
farming.whatIsDescription: Farming is the Survival mode economy where you till soil, plant seeds, water crops, harvest produce, and trade with the Trader, while defending your fields from escalating bot raids.

whySteps.step1Description: Till soil, place seeds, and set up manual or automated watering
whySteps.step2Description: Understand raid triggers, enemy waves, and crop defense tactics
whySteps.step3Description: Pack and sell harvests at the Packing Station for valuable crates
